title: Java Nested Classes
description: Practice static nested classes, inner classes, local classes, and anonymous classes.
learningGoals: Distinguish a static nested class from an inner class tied to an enclosing instance.
Recognize local and anonymous nested class objects in exam code.

completionChecks.prompt: Which nested class object can be created without an existing Order instance?
choices: AuditTask, because it is declared inside printAudit
Order.Summary, because it is a static nested class
The anonymous Runnable, because anonymous classes are always static
completionChecks.explanation: A static nested class is associated with the enclosing class, not an enclosing object. Inner, local, and anonymous classes in this lesson are created in an instance context.

completionChecks.prompt: What does printAudit() print for new Order("A17", 3)?
choices: A17 has 3 items, audit A17, async A17
A17 has 0 items, audit null, async null
Compilation fails because local classes cannot read fields
completionChecks.explanation: The inner ItemView, local AuditTask, and anonymous Runnable all run inside an Order instance and can read the instance field id; ItemView also reads itemCount.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if outside code writes new Order.ItemView()?
choices: It creates an ItemView with null id
It creates an ItemView only after Summary is initialized
Compilation fails because ItemView is a non-static inner class and needs an Order instance
It compiles because every nested class is implicitly static
completionChecks.explanation: ItemView is an inner class, not a static nested class. Code outside Order needs an enclosing instance, such as order.new ItemView(), before it can create one.
